requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into effect immediately upon issuance.
requirements.object: upon issuance

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Commercial Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
requirements.object: Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall bear
the meaning assigned to them in the ensuing paragraphs.
requirements.object: to them in the ensuing paragraphs

requirements.full_sentence:
(1) 1[*****]
(2) 2[*****]
3[(2A) "Acquisition Finance" shall have the same meaning as defined in the
Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2B) "Bridge Finance" shall have the same meaning as defined in the Reserve
Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2C) "Capital Market Intermediaries (CMIs)" shall have the same meaning as
defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(2D) "Collateral Security" or "Collateral" shall have the same meaning as
defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities)
Directions, 2025.]
(3) 4"Eligible capital base" means the effective amount of Tier 1 capital fulfilling
the criteria defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential
Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025, as per the last audited balance
sheet.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
date may also be taken into account.
requirements.object: account

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall obtain an external auditor's
certificate on completion of the augmentation of capital and submit the same to the
RBI (DOS, CO) before reckoning the additions to capital funds.
requirements.object: certificate on completion of the augmentation of capital and submit the same to the RBI (DOS, CO) before reckoning the ad

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, for an
Indian bank, profit accrued during the year, subject to provisions contained in
Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital
Adequacy) Directions, 2025, shall also be reckoned as Tier I capital for this purpose.
requirements.object: Tier I capital for this purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 5[*****]
(5) "Group" for the purpose of intragroup transactions and exposures (ITE)
shall have the following definition:

(i) "Group" shall be defined as an arrangement involving two or more entities
related to each other through any of the following relationships (subsidiary,
associate, joint venture and related party as defined in the applicable
accounting standards) and a 'group entity' as any entity involved in this
arrangement.
requirements.object: arrangement involving two or more entities related to each other through any of the following relationships (subsidiary, associate, j

requirements.full_sentence:
The other
relationships as defined in the paragraph 4(5)(ii) may, however, be
applicable for identifying entities of each public sector banking group
separately.
requirements.object: for identifying entities of each public sector banking group separately

requirements.full_sentence:
Exposures of a bank
to these entities shall be subject to the extant exposure limits stipulated by
the RBI.
requirements.object: extant exposure limits stipulated by the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) 6[The branches in other jurisdictions being part of a parent bank's
operations shall not be covered under the intra-group exposure limits
stipulated in paragraph 116.
requirements.object: intra-group exposure limits stipulated in paragraph 116

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly, an Indian bank's exposure to its
overseas branches and a foreign bank's (operating as branches in India)
exposure to its Head Office and overseas branches of the Head Office in
any jurisdiction shall not be covered under the ITE norms.
requirements.object: ITE norms

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) 7[*****]
(8) 8[*****]
(9) 9[*****]
10[(9A) "Non-debt Mutual Funds" shall mean mutual fund schemes corpus of
which are not exclusively invested in debt securities.
requirements.object: corpus of which are not exclusively invested in debt securities

requirements.full_sentence:
(9B) "Primary Security" shall have the same meaning as defined in the Reserve
Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.]
(10) "Qualifying Central Counterparty" or "QCCP" is an entity that is licensed to
operate as a central counterparty (CCP), including a license granted by way of
confirming an exemption, and is permitted by the appropriate regulator / overseer
to operate as such with respect to the products offered.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank
of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory mod

requirements.full_sentence:
13[Banks shall have policies on Concentration Risk Management of their exposures
towards a single counterparty, groups of interconnected counterparties, specific sectors of
the economy as also systems to monitor and address the risks emanating to them from
their exposures to ultra-large borrowers who are excessively leveraged and have
substantial borrowings from the banking system.
requirements.object: Risk Management of their exposures towards a single counterparty, groups of interconnected counterparties, specific sec

requirements.full_sentence:
While banks can have their own criteria
for deciding an ultra-large borrower, they shall take into account inter alia the overall
borrowings of such entities from the banking system for credit assessment of such
borrowers.] A list of the Board approved policies / limits to be formulated / set by the bank
as well as reviews to be carried out by / put up to the Board are given below, with the details
outlined in the ensuing paragraphs.
requirements.object: alia the overall borrowings of such entities from the banking system for credit assessment of such borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Review by / reporting to the Board - The following reviews and reporting shall
be put up to the Board:
requirements.object: the Board:

requirements.full_sentence:
Large Exposures Framework (LEF) shall be applied by a Commercial Bank at the same
level as the risk-based capital requirements are required to be applied i.e., at the following
two levels:
(1) Consolidated (Group) level: A bank shall apply LEF at the consolidated group
level, after consolidating the assets and liabilities of its subsidiaries / joint ventures /
associates (including overseas operations through bank's branches), excluding those
engaged in insurance and any non-financial activities.
requirements.object: Commercial Bank at the same level as the risk-based capital requirements are required to be applied i

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposures of all entities within
the banking group that are under regulatory scope of consolidation shall be considered
and the aggregate of such exposures shall be compared with the banking group's eligible
consolidated capital base.
requirements.object: aggregate of such exposures shall be compared with the banking group's eligible consolidated capital base

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Solo level: A bank shall apply LEF at the standalone level also (including overseas
operations through branches), which should measure the exposures to a counterparty
based on its standalone capital strength and risk profile.
requirements.object: standalone level also (including overseas operations through branches), which should measure the exposures to a counterparty based on

requirements.full_sentence:
15[Explanation: Exposure of a foreign bank (operating in India as a branch) to its Head
Office (HO), or to a branch of its HO in any jurisdiction, shall not fall under this exclusion,
and the counterparty limits applicable for its HO exposures in terms of paragraph 84 of
these Directions, shall apply to such exposures in aggregate.]
(8) borrowers, to whom limits are authorised for food credit;
requirements.object: and the counterparty limits applicable for its HO exposures in terms of paragraph 84 of these Directions, shall apply to su

requirements.full_sentence:
However, a bank's exposure to an exempted entity which is hedged by a credit
derivative shall be treated as an exposure to the counterparty providing the credit protection
notwithstanding the fact that the original exposure is exempted.
requirements.object: exposure to the counterparty providing the credit protection notwithstanding the fact that the original exposure is exempted

requirements.full_sentence:
All exempted exposures shall be reported by a bank as required under regulatory
reporting specified in paragraph 14, if these exposures meet the criteria for definition of a
'Large Exposure' as per paragraph 4(6).
requirements.object: bank as required under regulatory reporting specified in paragraph 14

requirements.full_sentence:
Lending under Consortium Arrangements: The exposure limits shall also be
applicable to lending under Consortium / Multiple Banking / Syndication Arrangements.
requirements.object: lending under Consortium / Multiple Banking / Syndication Arrangements

conditions:
where LC is issued by the Head Office
or branch of the same bank
requirements.full_sentence:
where LC is issued by the Head Office
or branch of the same bank, then the exposure should be taken on the third party / borrower
and not on the LC issuing bank.
requirements.object: third party / borrower and not on the LC issuing bank

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of negotiations 'under reserve', the exposure
should be treated as on the borrower.
requirements.object: the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall report its large exposure to the RBI, Department of Supervision, Central
Office, (DOS, CO), as per the reporting template given in Annex - I.
requirements.object: to the RBI, Department of Supervision, Central Office, (DOS, CO), as per the reporting template given in Annex - I

requirements.full_sentence:
The reporting, inter-
alia, shall include the following:
(1) all exposures, measured as specified in paragraphs 32 to 85 of this Chapter with
values equal to or above 10 percent of the bank's eligible capital (i.e., meeting the
definition of a large exposure as per paragraph 4(6));
requirements.object: all exposures, measured as specified in paragraphs 32 to 85 of this Chapter with values equal to or above 10 percent of the

requirements.full_sentence:
The sum of all the exposure values of a bank to a single counterparty shall not be
higher than 20 percent of the bank's available eligible capital base at all times.
requirements.object: percent of the bank's available eligible capital base at all times

requirements.full_sentence:
In exceptional cases, the Board of the bank may allow an additional five percent
exposure of the bank's available eligible capital base.
requirements.object: percent exposure of the bank's available eligible capital base

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall lay down a
Board approved policy in this regard.
requirements.object: approved policy in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
The sum of all the exposure values of a bank to a group of connected counterparties
(as defined in paragraphs 19 to 31) shall not be higher than 25 percent of the bank's
available eligible capital base at all times.
requirements.object: percent of the bank's available eligible capital base at all times

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposures shall be measured as specified in paragraphs 32 to 85 of these
Directions.
requirements.object: in paragraphs 32 to 85 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that the LE limits will be modulated in case of certain
counterparties as mentioned in paragraphs 73 to 85 of these Directions.
requirements.object: LE limits will be modulated in case of certain counterparties as mentioned in paragraphs 73 to 85 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Any breach of the LE limits prescribed in paragraphs 15 and 16 above shall be under
exceptional conditions beyond the control of the bank, and shall be reported to the RBI
(DOS, CO) immediately and rapidly rectified.
requirements.object: beyond the control of the bank, and shall be reported to the RBI (DOS, CO) immediately and rapidly rectified

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall assess the relationship amongst counterparties with reference to
paragraphs 20(1) and 20(2) in order to establish the existence of a group of connected
counterparties.
requirements.object: counterparties with reference to paragraphs 20(1) and 20(2) in order to establish the existence of a group of connecte

requirements.full_sentence:
In assessing whether there is a control relationship between counterparties,
the bank shall automatically consider that the control relationship criterion
Explanation: For example, in specific cases where a special purpose entity (SPE) that is
controlled by another client (e.g.
requirements.object: control relationship criterion Explanation: For example, in specific cases where a special purpose entity (SPE) that i

requirements.full_sentence:
In establishing connectedness based on economic interdependence, a bank shall
consider, at a minimum, the following criteria:
(1) where 50 percent or more of one counterparty's gross receipts or gross expenditures
(on an annual basis) is derived from transactions with the other counterparty;
requirements.object: the following criteria: (1) where 50 percent or more of one counterparty's gross receipts or gross expenditures (on an annual

conditions:
where one counterparty has fully or partly guaranteed the exposure of the other
counterparty
if a claim occurs
requirements.object: is so significant that the guarantor is likely to default if a claim occurs
repaid

requirements.full_sentence:
In some cases, a bank may have exposures to a group of counterparties with specific
relationships or dependencies such that, were one of the counterparties to fail, all of the
counterparties would very likely fail.
requirements.object: group of counterparties with specific relationships or dependencies such that, were one of the counterparties to fail, all of the

requirements.full_sentence:
A group of this sort, referred to in this Chapter as a
group of connected counterparties, shall be treated as a single counterparty.
requirements.object: single counterparty

conditions:
if at least one of the following criteria is satisfied:
(1) Control relationship Criteria: where one of the counterparties
requirements.full_sentence:
Two or more natural or legal persons shall be deemed to be a group of connected
counterparties if at least one of the following criteria is satisfied:
(1) Control relationship Criteria: where one of the counterparties, directly or indirectly,
has control over the other(s) or the counterparties are, directly or indirectly, controlled by
a third party (a bank may or may not have exposure towards this third party).
requirements.object: a group of connected counterparties if at least one of the following criteria is satisfied: (1) Control relationship Criteria: where o

conditions:
Where one counterparty has fully or partly guaranteed the exposure of the other
counterparty
if a claim occurs
requirements.object: is so significant that the guarantor is likely to default if a claim occurs

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may consider parameters
like networth, EBITDA, liquid assets, etc., to assess whether the guarantor
will be in a position to honour the claim on an on-going basis.
requirements.object: EBITDA, liquid assets, etc

conditions: if B fails to buy
requirements.full_sentence:
Illustrative Example: When a significant part of product / output / services of
Company A is sold to Company B and there are no alternate buyers who can
be approached if B fails to buy, in such a case goods may remain unsold and
could lead to default in loan repayment by A.
requirements.object: lead to default in loan repayment by A

conditions:
if the criteria would be applicable to the
counterparties under consideration
requirements.full_sentence:
For deciding if the criteria would be applicable to the
counterparties under consideration, the bank may use financial criteria like
unsold inventory leading to operating loss / default in repayment as well as
subjective criteria like ability of the seller to find alternate buyer / market, R&D
capability of the seller, etc.
requirements.object: unsold inventory leading to operating loss / default in repayment as well as subjective criteria like ability of the selle
repaid

requirements.full_sentence:
SBLC / BG from Head Office / other overseas branch)
from which CRM benefits like shifting of exposure / risk weights, etc., are not derived, may
not be counted as an exposure on the CRM provider.
requirements.object: exposure on the CRM provider

requirements.full_sentence:
The restrictions on dealing with
certain type of instruments, assets and derivatives etc., which are currently in place shall
Explanation: In the case that the market value of the credit derivative is positive from the
perspective of the protection seller, such a positive market value shall be added to the
exposure of the protection seller to the protection buyer (counterparty credit risk;
requirements.object: that the market value of the credit derivative is positive from the perspective of the protection seller, such a positive mar

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of credit-linked notes (CLNs), the protection seller bank shall be required
to consider its positions both in the bond of the note issuer and in the underlying referenced
by the note.
requirements.object: its positions both in the bond of the note issuer and in the underlying referenced by the note

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure value of option under this
Chapter shall be based on the change(s) in option prices that would result from a default of
the respective underlying instrument.
requirements.object: change(s) in option prices that would result from a default of the respective underlying instrument

requirements.full_sentence:
The resulting positions in all
cases should be aggregated with those from other exposures.
requirements.object: from other exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
After aggregation, negative
net exposures shall be treated as zero.

Exposure values of a bank's investments in transactions (i.e., index positions,
securitisations, hedge funds or investment funds) shall be calculated applying the same
rules as for similar instruments in the banking book (see paragraphs 63 to 70).
requirements.object: same rules as for similar instruments in the banking book (see paragraphs 63 to 70)

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may consider factors like financial
strength of counterparty B to withstand the shock, its ability to find alternate
supplier in place of C, etc.
requirements.object: strength of counterparty B to withstand the shock, its ability to find alternate supplier in place of C, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Illustrative Example: Examples would include all such cases where
insolvency or default of one company may lead to the insolvency or default
of the other companies.
requirements.object: or default of the other companies

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may use criteria such as intercorporate
liabilities, significant trade receivables, etc.
requirements.object: intercorporate liabilities, significant trade receivables, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Illustrative Example: Company A and Company B rely on the same non-bank
source for their funding requirements and may not have access to alternative
sources of funds.
requirements.object: sources of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Economic interdependence with two different entities: If an entity (C) is
economically dependent on two (or more) other entities (A and B) then payment difficulty
of any one of the entities (A or B) may cause payment difficulties to dependent entity (C).
requirements.object: dependent entity (C)

requirements.full_sentence:
There may, however, be circumstances where some of these criteria do not
automatically imply an economic dependence that results in two or more counterparties
being connected.
requirements.object: where some of these criteria do not automatically imply an economic dependence that results in two or more counterparties

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the bank can demonstrate that a counterparty which is economically
closely related to another counterparty may overcome financial difficulties, or even the
second counterparty's default, by finding alternative business partners or funding
sources within an appropriate time period, the bank does not need to combine these
counterparties to form a group of connected counterparties.
requirements.object: that a counterparty which is economically closely related to another counterparty may overcome financial difficulties, or eve

requirements.full_sentence:
Relation between interconnectedness through control and
interconnectedness through economic dependency: Group of counterparties based on
control and economic interdependence shall be assessed separately.

However, there may
be situations where the two types of dependencies are interlinked and could therefore exist
within one group of connected counterparties in such a way that all relevant clients
constitute a single risk.
requirements.object: two types of dependencies are interlinked and could therefore exist within one group of connected counterparties in such a way

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, B1 should be part of two separate
groups of A and B.
requirements.object: separate groups of A and B

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Three different groups of (a) A, A1, A2, (b) B, B1, (c) A2, B1, may not be sufficient
as financial difficulties of A2 is likely to cause difficulties for B1 also which is
economically dependent on A2 (which in turn is dependent on A).
requirements.object: difficulties of A2 is likely to cause difficulties for B1 also which is economically dependent on A2 (which in turn is depe

requirements.full_sentence:
both are economically dependent on each other, which means that
financial difficulty at either entity could impact the other entity.
requirements.object: either entity could impact the other entity

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Downstream Contagion: Downstream contagion should be assumed when an
entity is economically dependent on another entity and is itself the head of a 'control
group'.
requirements.object: entity is economically dependent on another entity and is itself the head of a 'control group'

conditions: If the other entity is part of a group of connected clients
requirements.full_sentence:
If the other entity is part of a group of connected clients, the control group
of the economically dependent entity should then be included in the group of
connected counterparties to which the economic dependency relationship exists.
requirements.object: the group of connected counterparties to which the economic dependency relationship exists

conditions:
If A2 faces
financial difficulty
requirements.full_sentence:
If A2 faces
financial difficulty, it may impact B1 adversely, which then is likely to withdraw
resources from its controlled entities B2 and B3.
requirements.object: then is likely to withdraw resources from its controlled entities B2 and B3

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Upstream Contagion: On the other hand, upstream contagion of entities that
control the economically dependent entity should be assumed only when the
controlling entity is also economically dependent on the entity that constitutes the
economic link between the two controlling groups.
requirements.object: the controlling entity is also economically dependent on the entity that constitutes the economic link between the two controllin

conditions:
If a bank is not
having exposure to all the entities
requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Limitations in formulating groups of connected counterparties: If a bank is not
having exposure to all the entities, it may be difficult to accurately form group of
connected counterparties.
requirements.object: form group of connected counterparties

requirements.full_sentence:
Such groups shall be formed on best efforts basis and
the bank should take reasonable steps to collect and use relevant information;
requirements.object: efforts basis and the bank should take reasonable steps to collect and use relevant information

conditions:
If there are
interconnections among entities that are not clients of the bank
requirements.full_sentence:
If there are
interconnections among entities that are not clients of the bank, it may be difficult
for the bank to formulate correct groupings.
requirements.object: bank to formulate correct groupings

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the bank should incorporate
any information that may be available to it publicly or through other clients or
entities outside its clientele.
requirements.object: publicly or through other clients or entities outside its clientele

conditions: if a bank has exposure to A and B5 only
requirements.full_sentence:
For instance, in illustration shown below, if a bank has exposure to A and B5 only,
then it may be difficult to formulate correct groupings.
requirements.object: correct groupings

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall frame a Board-approved policy for determining connectedness using
the criteria mentioned above.
requirements.object: for determining connectedness using the criteria mentioned above

requirements.full_sentence:
Under LEF, an exposure to a counterparty shall constitute both on and off-balance
sheet exposures included in either the banking or trading book and instruments with
counterparty credit risk.
requirements.object: off-balance sheet exposures included in either the banking or trading book and instruments with counterparty credit risk

requirements.full_sentence:
As an alternative, a bank may consider the exposure value gross of specific
provisions and value adjustments.
requirements.object: gross of specific provisions and value adjustments

requirements.full_sentence:
Banking book and trading book OTC derivatives (and any other instrument
with counterparty credit risk): The exposure value for instruments which give rise to
counterparty credit risk and are not securities financing transactions, should be determined
as per the RBI's extant instructions for the counterparty credit risk contained in Reserve
Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: the RBI's extant instructions for the counterparty credit risk contained in Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Prudential

requirements.full_sentence:
Securities financing transactions (SFTs): A bank shall use the method they
currently use for calculating its risk-based capital requirements against SFTs.
requirements.object: currently use for calculating its risk-based capital requirements against SFTs

requirements.full_sentence:
The interbank exposures, except intra-day interbank exposures, shall be subject to
the large exposure limit of 25 percent of a bank's Tier 1 capital (also refer to paragraphs 82
to 85).
requirements.object: large exposure limit of 25 percent of a bank's Tier 1 capital (also refer to paragraphs 82 to 85)

requirements.full_sentence:
In stressed circumstances, the RBI may accept a breach of an interbank limit ex
post, in order to help ensure stability in the interbank market.
requirements.object: an interbank limit ex post, in order to help ensure stability in the interbank market

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall assign such
exposure amount, i.e., the amount invested in a particular structure, to specific
counterparties of the underlying assets following the LTA described below:
(1) Look-Through Approach - a flow chart
<image>
(2) Look-Through Approach - An Illustrative example
requirements.object: i

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Exposure to underlying 8 (which is less than 0.25 percent of eligible capital base)
may be counted as exposure on structure itself.
requirements.object: on structure itself

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may assign the exposure amount to the structure itself, defined as a
distinct counterparty, if it can demonstrate that the bank's exposure amount to each
underlying asset of the structure is smaller than 0.25 percent of its eligible capital base,
considering only those exposure to underlying assets that result from the investment in the
structure itself and using the exposure value calculated according to paragraphs 69 and
requirements.object: to the structure itself, defined as a distinct counterparty

requirements.full_sentence: In this case, a bank may not look through the structure to identify the underlying assets.
requirements.object: to identify the underlying assets

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall look through the structure to identify those underlying assets for
which the underlying exposure value is equal to or above 0.25 percent of its eligible capital
base.
requirements.object: to identify those underlying assets for which the underlying exposure value is equal to or above 0

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, the counterparty corresponding to each of the underlying assets shall
be identified so that these underlying exposures can be added to any other direct or indirect
<image>
exposure to the same counterparty.
requirements.object: these underlying exposures can be added to any other direct or indirect <image> exposure to the same counterparty

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank's exposure amount to the underlying assets
that are below 0.25 percent of the bank's eligible capital base may be assigned to the
structure itself (i.e.
requirements.object: structure itself (i

conditions:
Where the total amount of the bank's exposures to a structure does not exceed 0.25
percent of its eligible capital base
requirements.full_sentence:
If a bank is unable to identify the underlying assets of a structure:
(1) Where the total amount of the bank's exposures to a structure does not exceed 0.25
percent of its eligible capital base, it shall assign the total exposure amount to the
structure itself, as a distinct counterparty.
requirements.object: amount to the structure itself, as a distinct counterparty

conditions:
if the exposure to the structure equals or exceeds 0.25 percent of its
eligible capital base)
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Otherwise (i.e., if the exposure to the structure equals or exceeds 0.25 percent of its
eligible capital base), it shall assign this total exposure amount to the 'unknown client'.
requirements.object: amount to the 'unknown client'

requirements.full_sentence:
The large exposure limit shall apply on the aggregate of all such exposures to 'unknown
clients' as if they are a single counterparty.
requirements.object: of all such exposures to 'unknown clients' as if they are a single counterparty

conditions: Where the LTA is not required (paragraph 64)
requirements.full_sentence:
Where the LTA is not required (paragraph 64), a bank shall nevertheless be able to
demonstrate that regulatory arbitrage considerations have not influenced the decision
whether to look through or not - e.g.
requirements.object: demonstrate that regulatory arbitrage considerations have not influenced the decision whether to look through or not - e

conditions: If LTA need not be applied
requirements.full_sentence:
If LTA need not be applied, a bank's exposure to the structure shall be the nominal
amount it invests in the structure.
requirements.object: it invests in the structure

requirements.full_sentence:
Thus, a bank holding a ₹1 investment in a structure,
which invests in 20 assets each with a value of ₹5, shall assign an exposure of ₹0.05 to
each of the counterparties.

An exposure to such counterparty shall be added to any other
direct or indirect exposures the bank has to that counterparty.
requirements.object: other direct or indirect exposures the bank has to that counterparty

conditions:
When the LTA (in terms of paragraphs above) is required for an
investment in a structure with different levels of seniority
requirements.full_sentence:
securitisation vehicles): When the LTA (in terms of paragraphs above) is required for an
investment in a structure with different levels of seniority, the exposure value to a
counterparty should be measured for each tranche within the structure, assuming a pro
rata distribution of losses amongst investors in a single tranche.
requirements.object: tranche within the structure, assuming a pro rata distribution of losses amongst investors in a single tranche

requirements.full_sentence:
To compute the exposure
value to the underlying asset, a bank shall:
(1) first, consider the lower of the value of the tranche in which the bank invests and the

While taking exposures to structures, a bank should identify such third parties which
may constitute an additional risk factor, and which are inherent in the structure itself rather
than in the underlying assets.

requirements.full_sentence:
RBI as a part of its pillar 2
supervisory review and evaluation process will look into this aspect and if required specify
a specific course of action which may either include reduction in exposure or raising of

requirements.full_sentence: The bank may consider multiple third parties to be potential drivers of additional risk.

requirements.full_sentence:
In this case, the bank shall assign the exposure resulting from the investment in the relevant

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank's exposures to QCCPs related to clearing activities shall be exempted from

However, these exposures shall be subject to the regulatory reporting

capital base.
requirements.object: a sum of both the clearing exposures described in paragraph 76 and the non-clearing exposures described in paragraph 78, and t

The concept of connected counterparties described in paragraphs 19 to 31 shall not
apply in the context of exposures to CCPs that are specifically related to clearing activities.
requirements.object: of exposures to CCPs that are specifically related to clearing activities

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall identify exposures to a CCP related to clearing activities and sum

Regarding exposures subject to clearing services (the bank acting as a clearing

requirements.
requirements.object: which exposures should be assigned by applying the provisions of the risk-based capital requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
Other types of exposures that are not directly related to clearing services provided

counterparty.
requirements.object: the rules set out in these Directions, as for any other type of counterparty

conditions:
if equity stakes in the CCP are deducted from the capital on which the

These exposures shall be added together and be subjected to the LE limit of
25 percent of the eligible capital base
Provided that, if equity stakes in the CCP are deducted from the capital on which the
large exposure limit is based, these shall not be included as exposure to the CCP.
requirements.object: to the CCP

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to limiting exposures to a single borrower or a group of borrowers, a bank

requirements.object: for aggregate commitments to specific sectors, e

requirements.full_sentence:
These sectoral limits shall be
fixed based on the bank's assessment of sectoral performance and associated risk

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall review and revise these limits periodically, as deemed

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall have Board approved limits in respect of various sub-segments under

In particular, limits shall be prescribed for all unsecured consumer credit

requirements.full_sentence:
The limits so fixed shall be strictly adhered to and monitored on an ongoing

requirements.object: and monitored on an ongoing basis by the Risk Management Committee

requirements.full_sentence:
All top-up loans extended by a bank against movable assets which are inherently
depreciating in nature, such as vehicles, shall be treated as unsecured loans for credit
appraisal, prudential limits and exposure purposes.
requirements.object: loans for credit appraisal, prudential limits and exposure purposes

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may also consider fixing internal limits for its aggregate exposure to all

requirements.object: limits for its aggregate exposure to all NBFCs put together

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should have an internal sub-limit on its aggregate exposures to all NBFCs,

together.
requirements.object: on its aggregate exposures to all NBFCs, having gold loans to the extent of 50 percent or more of their total financial ass

requirements.full_sentence:
This sub-limit should be within the internal limit, where fixed by the bank for its

requirements.object: limit

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall be guided by the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit

requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025
exposures and indirect exposures (both fund based, and non-fund based), including the following: (1) Investment Exposures: dir

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that, intraday limits to non-debt mutual fund schemes, which are
permitted to borrow intra-day only to the extent of guaranteed receivables due on
the same day on account of (a) maturity proceeds of Government Securities, T-
Bills, SDL or interest from G-Sec and SDLs held by such mutual funds, or (b)
maturity proceeds of TREPS from CCIL, shall not be reckoned as CME.

In terms of Section 19(2) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, no banking company
shall hold shares in any company, whether as pledgee, mortgagee or absolute owner, of
an amount exceeding 30 percent of the paid-up share capital of that company or 30 percent
of its own paid-up share capital and reserves, whichever is less, except as provided in sub-
section (1) of Section 19 of the Act.
requirements.object: company, whether as pledgee, mortgagee or absolute owner, of an amount exceeding 30 percent of the paid-up share capital of that company or 30 percent of its own paid-up share capital and reserves, whichever is less

requirements.object: the purpose of determining the exposure limit

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall strictly adhere to these statutory provisions while granting any

requirements.full_sentence:
Aggregate CME of a bank shall be subject to the following prudential ceilings ('CME
ceilings'), subject to the exclusions and qualifications as specified in subsequent
paragraphs, to be maintained on an ongoing basis:
(1) The aggregate CME of a bank, on both solo and consolidated basis, shall not
exceed 40 per cent of its eligible capital base.
requirements.object: of its eligible capital base

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A bank's direct capital market exposure, consisting of investment exposures as
per paragraph 95A, shall not exceed 20 per cent of eligible capital base on both
solo and consolidated basis.
requirements.object: of eligible capital base on both solo and consolidated basis

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A bank's aggregate exposure to acquisition finance shall not exceed 20 per cent
of its eligible capital base, within the aggregate CME ceiling of 40 per cent, both
on a solo and consolidated basis.
requirements.object: of its eligible capital base, within the aggregate CME ceiling of 40 per cent, both on a solo and consolidated basis

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Within its aggregate CME limit, a bank shall have a separate sub-limit for intra-
day exposure to a single counterparty, as well as an aggregate limit for all intra-
day exposures.
requirements.object: for intra- day exposure to a single counterparty, as well as an aggregate limit for all intra- day exposures

A bank shall adhere to the ceilings on an ongoing basis.]
100.

(1) Direct investment shall be calculated at its cost price.

clients, provided the borrower has expected receivables from a QCCP fully

A bank or its subsidiary shall not offer 'Safety Net' or any such facilities, which would

during a stipulated period, irrespective of the prevailing market price.

If a bank or its subsidiary provides a buy back arrangement to small investors
subscribing to new issues, such an arrangement shall not provide commitments to buy the

Prices should be determined from time to time, keeping
in view the prevailing stock market prices for the securities.

Commitments should also be
limited to a moderate proportion of the total issue in terms of the amount and should not

These commitments

However, exposure on account of equity and other regulatory capital instruments should

A bank shall adhere to the following intra-group exposure limits:
(1) Single Group Entity Exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Directions, 2025, shall continue to apply on such exposure, subject to the prohibitions

However,
prudential limits in respect of outstanding borrowing transactions in call / notice money

India (Call, Notice and Term Money Markets) Directions, 2021.
requirements.object: by Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Call, Notice and Term Money Markets) Directions, 2021

requirements.full_sentence:
For outstanding lending
transactions in the call / notice / term money market, the limits shall be decided by the
bank with the approval of its Board within the large exposure limits prescribed for single
counterparty / group of connected counterparties under LEF.
requirements.object: bank with the approval of its Board within the large exposure limits prescribed for single counterparty / group of connected counter

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall put in place a Board-approved comprehensive policy on monitoring and
management of ITEs.
requirements.object: Board-approved comprehensive policy on monitoring and management of ITEs

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy should lay down effective systems and processes to
identify, assess and report risk concentrations and material ITEs.
requirements.object: and processes to identify, assess and report risk concentrations and material ITEs

requirements.full_sentence:
While framing such policy,
the Board shall take into consideration the risks posed to the bank on a standalone basis
as a result of such intra-group activities and ensure that exposure to group entities are
appropriately captured in measures of the bank's exposures to group entities.
requirements.object: risks posed to the bank on a standalone basis as a result of such intra-group activities and ensure that exposure to group

requirements.full_sentence: The policy should be reviewed at least annually.
requirements.object: annually

conditions: if any, emanating there-from
requirements.full_sentence:
The policy should, at a minimum,
include:
<image>
(1) System of regular review and reporting of material ITEs to the Board for facilitating
clear understanding of the ITEs undertaken and the risks, if any, emanating there-from;
requirements.object: include: <image> (1) System of regular review and reporting of material ITEs to the Board for facilitating clear understanding of the ITEs undertaken and the risks

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) A requirement that the bank should address risks arising from ITEs as strictly as it
would address its risk exposures to a third party / non-group entity;

the purpose of supporting the business of group entities unless they are carried out at arm's length and in accord

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) The bank's material intra-group transactions (both fund-based and non-fund based)
should be examined by its internal auditors and the same should be checked by statutory
auditors on a sample basis to ascertain that intra-group transactions undertaken:
requirements.object: internal auditors and the same should be checked by statutory auditors on a sample basis to ascertain that intra-group transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Where the terms and conditions applying to a bank's dealings with group entities are
inconsistent with the benchmarks set for the similarly rated third party / non-group entities
as required under paragraph 120(3), they shall be put up to the Board by the sanctioning
authority with justifications.
requirements.object: the Board by the sanctioning authority with justifications

requirements.full_sentence:
The same may be made available to the RBI at the time of
inspection or whenever required.
requirements.object: the RBI at the time of inspection or whenever required

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall not enter into cross-default clauses whereby a default by a group entity
on an obligation (whether financial or otherwise) is deemed to trigger a default of the bank
on its obligations.
requirements.object: whereby a default by a group entity on an obligation (whether financial or otherwise) is deemed to trigger a default

conditions:
when they are done in accordance with the extant
instructions of RBI
requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should not buy / sell low quality asset (i.e., asset overdue / out of order or
classified as NPA by the bank or by RBI or a restructured asset whose terms have been
renegotiated or compromised due to the deteriorating financial condition of the borrower)
from / to group entities except when they are done in accordance with the extant
instructions of RBI, such as sale of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) to Asset Reconstruction
Companies, etc.
requirements.object: quality asset (i

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, a low-quality asset should not be accepted as collateral for a loan
or extension of credit to, or guarantee, acceptance, or letter of credit issued on behalf of
the group entity.
requirements.object: for a loan or extension of credit to, or guarantee, acceptance, or letter of credit issued on behalf of the group entity

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall also ensure that the transactions in low-quality assets with
group entities, whether regulated or unregulated, are not done for the purpose of hiding
losses or window dressing of balance sheets.
requirements.object: transactions in low-quality assets with group entities, whether regulated or unregulated, are not done for the purpose of hiding

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall ensure that it has adequate systems and controls in place for
identifying, monitoring, managing and reviewing exposures arising from ITEs.
requirements.object: adequate systems and controls in place for identifying, monitoring, managing and reviewing exposures arising from ITEs

requirements.full_sentence:
The RBI may
require a bank to put in place additional internal controls and a more robust risk monitoring,
managing, reporting and review mechanism on ITEs.
requirements.object: put in place additional internal controls and a more robust risk monitoring, managing, reporting and review mechanism on ITEs

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide support to its group entities so long as such support is
undertaken in accordance with the prudential requirements set out in above paragraphs in
relation to the policies governing bank's dealings with group entities.
requirements.object: group entities so long as such support is undertaken in accordance with the prudential requirements set out in above paragraphs

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank
should take ample and cautious measures to avoid giving any impression of its direct or
indirect support to group entities unless there are formal legal arrangements in place
providing for such support.
requirements.object: measures to avoid giving any impression of its direct or indirect support to group entities unless there are formal legal arra

requirements.full_sentence:
While assessing funding needs (especially under stressed situations), the bank
should account for any funding or liquidity commitment provided to group entities (e.g.
requirements.object: or liquidity commitment provided to group entities (e

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
be guided by the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Asset Liability Management)
Directions, 2025 in this regard.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Asset Liability Management) Directions, 2025 in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall also analyse how the liquidity positions of
group entities may affect its own liquidity, either through direct financial impact or through
contagion when those entities are faced with liquidity crunch.
requirements.object: liquidity positions of group entities may affect its own liquidity, either through direct financial impact or through contagion w

conditions:
Where there is reliance on
funding support among group entities
requirements.full_sentence:
Where there is reliance on
funding support among group entities, it should take into account legal, regulatory or other
<image>
limitations that may restrict group entities access to liquidity from it and vice versa in case
of need.
requirements.object: to liquidity from it and vice versa in case of need

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should establish internal limits on intra-group liquidity support to mitigate
the risk of contagion from other group entities when these entities are under liquidity stress.
requirements.object: intra-group liquidity support to mitigate the risk of contagion from other group entities when these entities are under l

requirements.full_sentence:
It may put in place group-wide contingency funding plans, liquidity cushions and diversified
funding to help group entities when liquidity problems in the group arise in line with the
guidelines referred to in paragraph 126.
requirements.object: contingency funding plans, liquidity cushions and diversified funding to help group entities when liquidity problems in the gr

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall submit the following data / information to the RBI (DOS, CO) in the
format and at the frequency prescribed by the DoS.
requirements.object: / information to the RBI (DOS, CO) in the format and at the frequency prescribed by the DoS

requirements.full_sentence: (1) The bank shall prepare and submit a list of the group entities.
requirements.object: list of the group entities

requirements.full_sentence:
The list shall include
all group entities established and operating in India and those overseas entities with
which it has material transactions (threshold for 'material' transaction would be ₹10 crore
for fund-based transactions and ₹25 crore for non-fund based transaction) during last
three financial years.
requirements.object: established and operating in India and those overseas entities with which it has material transactions (threshold for 'mate

requirements.full_sentence:
Any exclusion and / or inclusion of group entities should be reported
at the earliest.
requirements.object: earliest

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank shall submit the details of intra-group support arrangements / agreements
(e.g.
requirements.object: intra-group support arrangements / agreements (e

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The bank shall operate within the stipulated limits on an ongoing basis and report its
intra-group exposures.
requirements.object: limits on an ongoing basis and report its intra-group exposures

conditions: If the intra-group exposures
requirements.full_sentence:
(4) If the intra-group exposures, either at the single entity level or at the aggregate level,
exceed the prudential limits, the same should be reported at the earliest as also in the
prescribed returns along with the reasons for breach of limits.
requirements.object: earliest as also in the prescribed returns along with the reasons for breach of limits

requirements.full_sentence:
In such situations, the
bank cannot undertake any further intra-group exposure (at the entity or aggregate level,
as the case may be) until it is brought down within the limit.
requirements.object: brought down within the limit

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank, on
satisfactory grounds, may be allowed an appropriate timeline within which it should
comply with the stipulated limits.
requirements.object: timeline within which it should comply with the stipulated limits

conditions:
If the limits
are breached on account of mark-to-market values of derivatives position
requirements.full_sentence:
Failure to comply with the intra-group exposure limit
within the given timeline would result in deduction of excess exposure amount from
Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital of bank until the limits are restored 34[(If the limits
are breached on account of mark-to-market values of derivatives position, the excess
exposure would not be deducted from CET1 capital for a period of three months from
the date of breach.)] The frequent breaches may also lead to imposition of penalties on
the bank by the RBI.
requirements.object: of penalties on the bank by the RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall refer to the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Risk
Management) Directions, 2025 for detailed instructions on Country Risk Management.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Commercial Banks - Credit Risk Management) Directions, 2025 for detailed instructions on Country Risk Management

conditions: if considered necessary for products
requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of a bank shall set country exposure limits in relation to the bank's
regulatory capital (Tier I + Tier II) with sub-limits, if considered necessary for products,
branches, maturity etc.
requirements.object: in relation to the bank's regulatory capital (Tier I + Tier II) with sub-limits

requirements.full_sentence:
The basis for setting the limits for the country / category shall be left
to the discretion of the banks' Boards.
requirements.object: discretion of the banks' Boards

requirements.full_sentence:
The country exposure limits set by the Board shall
be reviewed periodically, and in any case, at least once a year.
requirements.object: in any case, at least once a year

requirements.full_sentence:
Exposure limit for any country shall not exceed its regulatory capital, except in the
case of insignificant risk category.

In respect of foreign banks, the regulatory capital shall
be the capital (Tier I + Tier II) held in their Indian books.
requirements.object: I + Tier II) held in their Indian books

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may also set up regional exposure limits for country groups, at the discretion
of its Board.
requirements.object: exposure limits for country groups, at the discretion of its Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall decide on the basis for grouping of countries and also lay
down the guidelines regarding all aspects of such regional exposure limits.
requirements.object: for grouping of countries and also lay down the guidelines regarding all aspects of such regional exposure limits

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should reckon both funded and non-funded exposures from their domestic as
well as foreign branches while identifying, measuring, monitoring and controlling country
risks.
requirements.object: non-funded exposures from their domestic as well as foreign branches while identifying, measuring, monitoring and controlling c

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should take into account indirect country risk where its net funded exposure
to a country is one per cent or more of its total assets.
requirements.object: country risk where its net funded exposure to a country is one per cent or more of its total assets

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank is required to reckon such
country risk exposures for measuring, monitoring and controlling with that country risk.
requirements.object: risk exposures for measuring, monitoring and controlling with that country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
For
example, exposures to a domestic commercial borrower with a large economic
dependence on a certain country may be considered as subject to indirect country risk.
requirements.object: to indirect country risk

requirements.full_sentence:
Indirect exposures may be reckoned at 50% of the exposure for the purpose of these
guidelines.
requirements.object: of the exposure for the purpose of these guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
Exposures should be computed on a net basis i.e., gross exposure 'minus'
collaterals, guarantees, insurance etc.
requirements.object: net basis i

requirements.full_sentence:
Netting may be permitted for cash collaterals, bank
guarantees and credit insurance available in/ issued by countries in a lower risk category
than the country on which exposure is assumed.
requirements.object: collaterals, bank guarantees and credit insurance available in/ issued by countries in a lower risk category than the country on

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the
issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to be
governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted under these
repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and
any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications
in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall be final and binding.
